name: Collado Ormazabal, Cabeza Parda, Collado de Beneguerra – circular route
name_original: Collado Ormazabal, Cabeza Parda, Collado de Beneguerra – ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking around Ezcaray offers access to the Sierra de la Demanda mountains in La Rioja, Spain. The region features high peaks, the Oja River valley, and extensive forests, providing diverse terrain for outdoor exploration. Trails traverse varied landscapes, from riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. This area is characterized by its natural setting and a network of well-maintained routes. Best hiking trails around Ezcaray The most popular hiking route is El Hombre Circular Trail from Ezcaray, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local hikers is Collado Ormazabal, Cabeza Parda, Collado de Beneguerra – circular route, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This trail navigates through mountain passes and offers views of prominent peaks. Local hikers also love the Ezcaray – Turza Village loop from Ezcaray, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the Oja River valley and local villages, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Ezcaray is defined by the Sierra de la Demanda mountains, the Oja River, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to demanding ascents. The routes in Ezcaray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ezcaray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ezcaray?
A: Ezcaray offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 70 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to all skill levels, from easy strolls along the Oja River to challenging ascents in the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for families or beginners in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, Ezcaray has several easy hiking options perfect for families or those new to hiking. There are 12 easy routes available, many of which follow the picturesque Oja River valley or lead through charming local villages. These trails allow you to enjoy the natural beauty without demanding ascents.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Ezcaray?
A: Absolutely. Many of Ezcaray's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ezcaray – Turza Village loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11 km path that takes you through the Oja River valley and local villages, offering a pleasant round trip.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see on the trails around Ezcaray?
A: Hiking around Ezcaray immerses you in diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter the high peaks of the Sierra de la Demanda, the lush Oja River valley, and extensive ancient forests, which make up a significant portion of the region. The area is known for its impressive mountain scenery and serene river environments.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, many trails lead to or pass by interesting landmarks. You can hike to historical hermitages like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10463642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Nuestra Señora de Allende&lt;/a&gt;, which offers cultural interest and scenic viewpoints. The region also features significant religious sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4598570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2918989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán de la Cogolla Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as part of longer excursions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ezcaray?
A: The hiking trails in Ezcaray are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the well-maintained paths, the stunning mountain views, and the peaceful atmosphere of the forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Ezcaray area?
A: For experienced trekkers, Ezcaray offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989427317/el-hombre-circular-trail-from-ezcaray" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hombre Circular Trail from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 16.9 km route with nearly 800 meters of ascent, providing panoramic views of the Sierra de la Demanda. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10463853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo (2,271 m) – San Lorenzo loop from Urdanta&lt;/a&gt;, which involves over 1200 meters of climbing.

Q: Is Ezcaray a good destination for winter hiking?
A: Ezcaray can be a beautiful destination for winter hiking, especially with the nearby Valdezcaray Ski Resort. However, winter conditions in the Sierra de la Demanda can be challenging, with snow and ice at higher elevations. It's crucial to check weather forecasts, be prepared with appropriate gear like snowshoes or microspikes, and be aware of potential avalanche risks. Lower elevation trails along the Oja River might offer more accessible winter walks.

Q: Are there any trails that offer exceptional viewpoints in Ezcaray?
A: Many trails in Ezcaray provide breathtaking viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989598370/collado-ormazabal-cabeza-parda-collado-de-beneguerra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Ormazabal, Cabeza Parda, Collado de Beneguerra – circular route&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views of prominent peaks and mountain passes. Additionally, the Hermitage of Santa Bárbara, located atop a mountain, provides panoramic vistas of the valley and surrounding mountains.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ezcaray?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Ezcaray region. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there options for food and drink along the hiking routes or in Ezcaray?
A: Ezcaray itself is known for its hospitality and offers various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. While most trails don't have facilities directly on them, some routes pass through small villages where you might find a local establishment. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ezcaray?
A: The best time to hike in Ezcaray largely depends on your preferred conditions. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, vibrant foliage, and blooming wildflowers, making them ideal for most hikers. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but higher mountain trails provide cooler air. Winter offers a unique experience with snow, but requires more preparation and caution.
tips: Close to the Valdezcaray ski resort (to the west) and the GR-190 (500m to the east).
Cabeza Parda, with an altitude of 2,106 meters, surpasses other peaks in the area, so the views are very good.
Dividing mountain pass between the Oja and Cárdenas valleys. Crossing point of the Gr 190, Altos Valles Ibéricos, Ezcaray Valvanera section.

waypoints.name: View of Cabeza Parda from Collado Ormazabal
